zh.lifeSummary: John Farrar於1550年12月2日出生在英國越可爾哈利法可。他於大約1570年與Isabell Houghton在英國藍卡爾波利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1595年10月4日在英國英格蘭藍卡爾波利享年44歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Farrar于1550年12月2日出生在英国越可爾哈利法可。他于大约1570年与Isabell Houghton在英国藍卡爾波利结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1595年10月4日在英国英格兰藍卡爾波利享年44岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾波利。

nameMeanings.name: Farrar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): occupational name for a blacksmith or worker in iron. From Middle English fer(r)o(u)r, fer(r)er, fa(r)ro(u)r ‘ironworker, blacksmith’ (Old French ferreor, from medieval Latin ferrator, an agent derivative of ferrare ‘to shoe horses’, from ferrum ‘iron’, in medieval Latin ‘horseshoe’). Compare Ferrier and Farrow .

variants.name: Ferrer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Juan, Jorge, Carlos, Angel, Pedro, Roberto, Manuel, Ramon.
Catalan and southern French: from Latin ferrarius ‘ironworker, blacksmith’, either an occupational name or from the same word as an old personal name. This is the commonest Catalan surname.
English: variant of   Farrar  .

variants.name: Farrer
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .
German: variant of   Forer   or   Fahrer  .

variants.name: Ferrar
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .

variants.name: Ferro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Angelo, Mario, Pasquale, Alberto, Antonino, Bernardo, Guido, Carmine, Concetta, Dino, Ottavio, Pietro, Rocco.  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Luis, Carlos, Ramon, Eduardo, Fermin, Sixto, Angel.

Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, and Maltese: from ferro ‘iron’ (from Latin ferrum), used as a nickname denoting someone who was strong or as a metonymic occupational name for someone who produced or worked iron.
Italian: from a medieval personal name, preserved in the modern pet form Ferruccio.

variants.name: Fara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Eduardo, Carlos, Mario.
Italian (Sardinia): occupational name from Sardinian fara ‘messenger, courier, collector’.
Czech and Slovak (also Fára): topographic name from fara ‘priest's house’.

variants.name: Farrow
meanings: English (northern): hypercorrected form of Farrar , an occupational name for a smith or worker in iron. The original -ar or -er ending of this name came to be regarded as an error, and was changed to -ow.

variants.name: Farrah
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .
Americanized form of Arabic   Farah  .

variants.name: Ferrier
meanings: Scottish and English: occupational name for a smith, one who shoed horses, from Old French ferrier ‘blacksmith, ironworker’ (medieval Latin ferrarius, from ferrus ‘horseshoe’, from Latin ferrum ‘iron’). Compare Farrier , Farrar .

Scottish and English: occupational name for a ferryman, from Middle English ferier(e) ‘ferryman’. Black reports that lands called Ferrylands in Dumbarton, by a ferry across the Clyde, belonged to Robert Ferrier in 1512.
French: occupational name from Old French ferrier ‘blacksmith, farrier’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Farra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ammar, Bassel, Fadi, Fadia, Farid, Hazem, Nabil, Nadim, Salem, Samir, Waseem, Yasser.  French Henri, Marcel.
English: variant of   Farrar  .
Arabic (the Levant): possibly a variant of   Farah  . Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians.

variants.name: Furrow
meanings: Probably an altered form of English Farrow (see Farrar ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: John Farrar was born on 2 December 1550, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England. He married Isabell Houghton about 1570, in Burnley, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 4 October 1595, in Burnley, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44, and was buried in Burnley, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: John Farrar est né le 2 décembre 1550, à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre. Il a épousé Isabell Houghton environ 1570, à Burnley, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 4 octobre 1595, à Burnley, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Burnley, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: John Farrar wurde am 2. Dezember 1550 in Halifax, Yorkshire, England geboren. Er hat um 1570 in Burnley, Lancashire, England Isabell Houghton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 4. Oktober 1595 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Burnley, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Burnley, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: John Farrar è nato il il 2 dicembre 1550 nel Halifax, Yorkshire, England. Lui sposato Isabell Houghton circa 1570, nel Burnley, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 4 ottobre 1595, nel Burnley, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Burnley, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John Farrarは1550年12月2日にイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスで生まれた。約1570年にイギリスランカシュアイアブルンリーでIsabell Houghtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1595年10月4日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアブルンリーで44歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアブルンリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Farrar(은)는 1550년 12월 2일 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1570년 영국 랭카실르 번리에서 Isabell Houghton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1595년 10월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 번리에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 번리에 묻혔다.

pt.2 Dec 1550–4 Oct 1595: 2 de dezembro de 1550–4 de outubro de 1595
pt.lifeSummary: John Farrar nasceu em 2 de dezembro de 1550, em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Isabell Houghton aproximadamente 1570, em Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de outubro de 1595, em Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: John Farrar родился 2 декабря 1550 г. в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabell Houghton около 1570 г. в местности бурнли, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 4 октября 1595 г. в местности бурнли, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности бурнли, ланкашир, Англия.

es.2 Dec 1550–4 Oct 1595: 2 de diciembre de 1550–4 de octubre de 1595
es.lifeSummary: John Farrar nació el 2 de diciembre de 1550, en Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Isabell Houghton aproximadamente 1570, en Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 4 de octubre de 1595, en Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
